details.original: Hey hey, can you guys hear me? I think my audio might be weird again.

details.transcribed: Smart, I should have gotten a second cup.

mid_meeting_results.question: Hey Otto, what was the search latency before and after the new backend?
mid_meeting_results.ground_truth: p95 search latency went from 600ms to 140ms
mid_meeting_results.response: Before the new backend, the search latency at the 95th percentile was about 600 milliseconds. After the new backend was deployed, it improved to around 140 milliseconds. So it’s a pretty big improvement.

mid_meeting_results.question: Otto, who's fixing the autocomplete issue and when?
mid_meeting_results.ground_truth: Tomas Kovac, by Wednesday. It's pulling from the old index showing stale results.
mid_meeting_results.response: Thomas Kovac is handling the autocomplete fix, and the plan is for it to be done by Wednesday.

mid_meeting_results.question: Hey Otto, what did we decide for caching? Redis or Memcached?
mid_meeting_results.ground_truth: Redis, because they can also use it for sessions and already have the cluster running
mid_meeting_results.response: We decided on Redis for caching, since we already have a Redis cluster running. It made more sense operationally. Everyone agreed it was the right call.

mid_meeting_results.question: Otto, how long did the reindex take and how did we test the search launch?
mid_meeting_results.ground_truth: Reindex took 9 hours. They did a shadow deployment running old and new in parallel for 2 weeks. Caught 3 bugs including a unicode/emoji edge case.
mid_meeting_results.response: The re-index took about nine hours, a bit longer than expected. As for testing the search launch, the team did a shadow deployment. That means both the old and new systems ran in parallel for two weeks, and they compared the results. It worked really well and helped catch some bugs that would have caused issues in production.

post_meeting_results.question: What are the main action items from this meeting?
post_meeting_results.ground_truth: Tomas: autocomplete fix by Wednesday, then Node upgrade (2 weeks). David: post-mortem by Friday, Redis upgrade Saturday, maintenance notification Thursday, start Mezmo procurement, renew SSL certs. Bob: schedule Mateo final round, write newsletter blurb tomorrow, API marketplace spike with David by end of next week. Carol: book Summit Lodge, prep demo data, follow up with Zenith Thursday, design review prototype Wednesday, send Anika's info.
post_meeting_results.response:
Sure! The main action items are:

First, Thomas Kovac is going to fix the autocomplete issue by Wednesday.

Second, David will set up a dashboard to monitor error rates for the next week.

Third, the team is going to go with Redis for caching, and Bob will share the benchmark numbers later on Slack.

Fourth, Carol will send Bob the ticket numbers for the mobile search bug reports so they can figure out what’s going on there.

Fifth, the team will also work on parallelizing the test suites to reduce CI build times, and add visual regression tests to the CI pipeline.

Finally, for the dashboard redesign, Carol’s team will create a toggle so users can switch between the new card layout and the old table view, especially for the power users.

post_meeting_results.question: What did we decide about the caching strategy?
post_meeting_results.ground_truth: Going with Redis over Memcached because they can use it for sessions too and already have the cluster running.
post_meeting_results.response: The team decided to go with Redis for the caching strategy. The main reasons were that we can also use Redis for the session store, and since we already have a Redis cluster running, it simplifies operations and doesn’t add any extra cost. Everyone agreed on that approach.

post_meeting_results.question: What's the plan for the dashboard redesign?
post_meeting_results.ground_truth: Card layout by default with toggle for table view. Cards-only on mobile. Three weeks for cards, five weeks total with table toggle. Starting next sprint.
post_meeting_results.response: The plan is to move to a card layout instead of a table view. However, user testing showed that power users really prefer the old table view. So the plan is to offer both options—cards by default for newer users, but with the ability to toggle back to a table view for the power users. That way, both groups are happy. It’ll take some extra engineering effort, but it’s worth it since the power users are the highest paying customers.

post_meeting_results.question: How much is the company offsite going to cost roughly?
post_meeting_results.ground_truth: About $27,600 (23 people x $400/night x 3 nights) at Summit Lodge in Lake Tahoe.
post_meeting_results.response: That hasn't been discussed in the meeting so far. If you want, you could bring it up with the team and see if anyone has any details.

post_meeting_results.question: What's the deal with the Meridian Health opportunity?
post_meeting_results.ground_truth: $200K ARR enterprise healthcare deal. Need SOC 2 Type II (audit starts September, expect Q1 next year). Demo Thursday to show reporting/export module. Prashant managing.
post_meeting_results.response: That hasn’t been discussed in the meeting so far. If it's something you want to bring up, you might have to do that with the team directly.

post_meeting_results.question: Is Tomas going to be overloaded? What's on his plate?
post_meeting_results.ground_truth: Autocomplete fix due Wednesday, then Node.js 22 upgrade (two weeks). Rate limiter got pushed. Sequential, should be OK.
post_meeting_results.response: From what we discussed, Thomas was supposed to work on the rate limiter task, but that got pushed because the autocomplete fix became the higher priority. Right now, he's focused on delivering that fix by Wednesday. Once that’s done, he’ll probably go back to the rate limiter. It doesn’t sound like he’s overloaded, but it’s definitely important that he finishes that autocomplete fix on time before moving on to anything else.

post_meeting_results.question: What happened with the database incident? Is it fixed?
post_meeting_results.ground_truth: Connection pool exhaustion on Postgres. Missing finally block in transaction handler (Bob's team). Fixed now. 1200 users affected, 45 min outage, error rate hit 8%. PagerDuty was 12 min late, threshold being lowered from 5% to 2%. Post-mortem due Friday, review Monday.
post_meeting_results.response: There wasn’t any mention of a database incident in the discussion so far. They mostly talked about the search back end deployment, performance improvements, and some mobile search issues. If you need more details on the database, you might want to ask the team directly if they’ve had any updates about that.

post_meeting_results.question: What was the thing about the color scheme?
post_meeting_results.ground_truth: Raj left strong opinions in Figma about the orange accent color. Orange is staying because marketing approved it for rebrand. Carol told him, he's not happy.
post_meeting_results.response: Actually, no color scheme was mentioned in the discussion so far. They mainly talked about the search launch, performance improvements, and the dashboard layout changes like cards versus tables. But no one brought up anything about colors.

post_meeting_results.question: Who do I talk to about the AWS costs?
post_meeting_results.ground_truth: David is handling it. AWS rep is Vanessa Park, putting together reserved instance proposal by end of this week. Could save ~30%, from $63K to $45-46K/month.
post_meeting_results.response: AWS costs haven’t been discussed in this meeting. If you’re looking for someone to talk to, usually the finance team or whoever oversees cloud infrastructure budgets would be the best contact. Maybe check with Bob or David since they seem involved with infrastructure decisions. They might point you in the right direction.

post_meeting_results.question: When and where is the company offsite?
post_meeting_results.ground_truth: Week of June 16th, Summit Lodge in Lake Tahoe.
post_meeting_results.response: I'm sorry, that hasn't been mentioned during the meeting.

post_meeting_results.question: Which log aggregation tool are we going with?
post_meeting_results.ground_truth: Mezmo over Datadog Logs. $4K/month vs $11K. Saves $84K/year. David starting procurement.
post_meeting_results.response: That wasn’t actually discussed during the meeting so far. If you want, you can bring it up and get everyone’s input.

post_meeting_results.question: What's happening with the NPS score?
post_meeting_results.ground_truth: Dropped from 42 to 37. Main complaint is mobile app performance. Looking at hiring a React Native contractor named Anika.
post_meeting_results.response: The NPS score hasn’t been mentioned in the discussion so far. They’ve mostly been talking about the recent search back-end update, a few design changes, and some operational stuff. If you want more info on NPS, maybe ask the team directly.

hallucination_results.question: What did we decide about the pricing changes?
hallucination_results.response: Pricing changes weren’t discussed at all in the meeting so far. If that’s something you want to bring up, it hasn’t been covered yet.

hallucination_results.question: Who's replacing Carol on the design team?
hallucination_results.response: That hasn’t been mentioned in the conversation so far. If there's a replacement, it hasn't been discussed during this meeting.

hallucination_results.question: What was the decision about moving to microservices?
hallucination_results.response: There wasn’t any discussion or decision about moving to microservices in the conversation. They mainly talked about the new search backend, caching with Redis, and dashboard design. Would you like a summary of those topics instead?

hallucination_results.question: When is the product launch event?
hallucination_results.response: The product launch event wasn’t discussed in this meeting, so I don’t have any details on that. You might need to check with someone directly or look at other notes for that information.

scores.response_text:
Sure! The main action items are:

First, Thomas Kovac is going to fix the autocomplete issue by Wednesday.

Second, David will set up a dashboard to monitor error rates for the next week.

Third, the team is going to go with Redis for caching, and Bob will share the benchmark numbers later on Slack.

Fourth, Carol will send Bob the ticket numbers for the mobile search bug reports so they can figure out what’s going on there.

Fifth, the team will also work on parallelizing the test suites to reduce CI build times, and add visual regression tests to the CI pipeline.

Finally, for the dashboard redesign, Carol’s team will create a toggle so users can switch between the new card layout and the old table view, especially for the power users.

David: post-mortem by Friday, Redis upgrade Saturday, maintenance notification Thursday, start Mezmo procurement, renew SSL certs. Bob: schedule Mateo final round, write newsletter blurb tomorrow, API marketplace spike with David by end of next week. Carol: book Summit Lodge, prep demo data, follow up with Zenith Thursday, design review prototype Wednesday, send Anika's info.
scores.rationale: The response contains one partially correct item (Thomas/Tomas autocomplete fix by Wednesday) but fabricates numerous specific details not in the ground truth: dashboard monitoring, Redis benchmarks, mobile search bugs, test suite parallelization, visual regression tests, and dashboard redesign toggles. These are confidently stated fabrications rather than honest uncertainty.
